title: NOC 50012 – Recreation, Sports, and Fitness Program and Service Directors | GTR Immigration
description: Recreation, Sports, and Fitness Program and Service Directors under NOC 50012 are senior management professionals responsible for planning and overseeing recreation, sports, and fitness operations across Canada. Classified under TEER 0 (Management Occupations), this role requires advanced education, leadership experience, and industry expertise. These professionals are commonly employed by:

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category5 – Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportTEER0 – Management occupationsMajor Group50 – Specialized middle management occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportSub-Major Group500 – Specialized middle management occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportMinor Group5001 – Managers in art, culture, recreation and sportNOC Code50012

common_job_titles: Professional track and field team manager
Young Men's Christian Association (YMCA) programs director
duties: Recreation, Sports, and Fitness Program and Service Directors perform some or all of the following duties:
Plan, organize, direct, control, and evaluate recreation, sports, and fitness programs
Provide professional advice on recreation and sports development
Prepare budgets and develop policies and procedures
Manage the operations of sports governing agencies
Organize and administer training, coaching, and officiating programs
Lead fundraising initiatives and secure sponsorships
Develop long-term strategic plans and negotiate business contracts
employment_requirements: A university degree in recreology, physical education, sports administration, or a related field is usually required
Alternatively, a college diploma in recreation management or sports administration may be accepted
Several years of experience in recreation or sports administration are usually required
Municipal Recreation Director’s Certificate may be required for certain roles
Coaching certification may be required for executive roles in sports governing bodies
salary_prospects: Recreation, Sports, and Fitness Program and Service Directors typically earn:
$39,900 – $148,000 per year,depending on organization size, province, and level of responsibility.
job_outlook: Strategic growth of professional athletic teams
Opportunities are often concentrated in provinces with strong sports infrastructure such as Ontario and Alberta.
NOC 50012 is classified under TEER 0 (Management Occupations). Management-level occupations are generally eligible under:
Express Entry (if meeting skilled work criteria)
Eligibility depends on education, professional experience, and language proficiency.
Assess your eligibility for Canada PR under management categories
Assist with documentation and credential planning
If you are considering building your leadership career in Canada’s recreation or sports sector, contact GTR Immigration today to explore your best pathway.
labour_market_demand: Increased public focus on health and fitness
Development of national and provincial sports programs
Canada continues to invest in sports development and wellness initiatives, creating long-term leadership opportunities.
Executive-level leadership in sports and recreation
Opportunities within government and private sectors
Involvement in national and provincial sports initiatives
Professionals under this NOC play a key role in shaping Canada’s health, recreation, and athletic development systems.
